title: My thoughts on AI art (a totally non-controversial topic)
body:
Daniel tackles the increasingly relevant topic of AI art with a nuanced perspective. While he firmly supports artists being compensated fairly for their work, he’s not entirely opposed to AI’s role in the creative process, especially as a tool for disabled individuals or in prototyping stages. He stresses that transparency from creators is essential—if AI is used, consumers should know. Daniel also highlights the complexity of researching every game for AI usage, noting it’s beyond his time and capacity. He rejects the toxic culture of AI witch hunts, stressing that policing AI use is not his channel’s role.

He acknowledges that AI-generated mediocrity flooding the market is a concern and calls for creators to maintain transparency. Ultimately, Daniel wants to focus on enjoying and reviewing games, while encouraging fair compensation for creators and understanding the challenges of regulating AI. His approach is open, recognizing that this issue will continue evolving as technology and attitudes shift.
